1385	\begin{definition}
1386	
1387	\leanok
1388	[Locally free of rank \(d\)]
1389	  \label{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
1390	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank}
1391	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises (2)" and
1392	  % "Grassmannian of a vector bundle" (read from
1393	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L562-L564
1394	  % + L584-L585).
1395	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1396	  % "$\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
1397	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
1398	  %  [...]
1399	  %  the set of all equivalence classes $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients
1400	  %  $q: E_T \to \F$ where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$,"
1401	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
1402	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be a sheaf of modules on a scheme \(X\) and let
1403	  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). We say \(\mathcal{M}\) is \emph{locally free of rank
1404	  \(d\)} when there exists an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) together with
1405	  isomorphisms \(\mathcal{M}|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\oplus d}\) for
1406	  each \(i\).
1407	
1408	  \emph{Note.} Mathlib does not provide this predicate.
1409	\end{definition}

1451	  %  This shows that the functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}\) naturally decomposes

1453	  %  \(\)\Quot_{E/X/S} = \coprod_{{\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]}\, \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\(\)
1454	  %  where for any polynomial \({\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]\), the functor
1455	  %  \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) associates
1456	  %  to any \(T\) the set of all equivalence classes of
1457	  %  families \(\langle \F,q \rangle\) such that at each \(t\in T\) the

1459	  %  using the pull-back of \(L\), is \({\Phi}\)."
1460	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
1461	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
1462	  \(L\) a line bundle on \(X\), \(E\) a coherent sheaf on \(X\), and
1463	  \(\Phi \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\). The \emph{Quot functor with Hilbert
1464	  polynomial \(\Phi\)}
1465	  \[
1466	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S} : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set}
1467	  \]
1468	  sends an \(S\)-scheme \(T \to S\) to the set of equivalence classes
1469	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) where
1470	  \begin{enumerate}
1471	    \item \(\mathcal{F}\) is a coherent sheaf on \(X_T = X \times_S T\) whose
1472	      schematic support is proper over \(T\) (\cref{def:has_proper_support}) and
1473	      which is flat over \(T\);
1474	    \item \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is a surjective
1475	      \(\mathcal{O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism, where \(E_T\) is the pull-back
1476	      of \(E\) along \(X_T \to X\);
1477	    \item for every \(t \in T\) the Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}\)
1478	      with respect to \(L|_{X_t}\) (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
1479	      \(\Phi\).
1480	  \end{enumerate}
1481	  Two pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) and \((\mathcal{F}', q')\) are equivalent iff
1482	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). On morphisms \(f : T' \to T\) over \(S\), the functor
1483	  acts by pulling back the quotient along \(X_{T'} \to X_T\) (well-defined
1484	  since tensor product is right-exact and preserves both flatness and
1485	  properness). The Hilbert scheme is the special case
1486	  \(\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} := \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\).
1487	\end{definition}

1489	\section{The Grassmannian scheme}
1490	\label{sec:grassmannian_scheme}
1491	
1492	The Quot construction proceeds by embedding \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) as a
1493	locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian over \(S\). Since Mathlib carries
1494	no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} (only a finite-rank-quotient variety in
1495	linear algebra), we package the construction here.

1534	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(V\) be a locally free
1535	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\). For an integer \(1 \le d \le r\), the
1536	  \emph{Grassmannian of rank-\(d\) quotients of \(V\)} is the functor
1537	  \[

1539	    \qquad

1626	  line bundle \(\det(\mathcal{U})\) is relatively very ample, and the
1627	  associated linear system gives a closed embedding

1633	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the "Construction of Grassmannian" sub-section of
1634	% [Nitsure], \S 1 (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
1635	% L773-L930), which gives the construction in five named steps
1636	% ("Construction by gluing together affine patches", "Separatedness",
1637	% "Properness", "Universal quotient", "Projective embedding"). The total
1638	% verbatim text is ~150 lines; it is reproduced piecewise in the
1639	% SOURCE QUOTE comment of the theorem statement above.
1640	\begin{proof}
1641	  
1642	  Over \(S = \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) and \(V = \mathcal{O}_S^{\oplus r}\) the
1643	  Grassmannian \(\mathrm{Gr}(r, d)\) is constructed by gluing
1644	  \(\binom{r}{d}\) affine charts \(U^I = \Spec\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) indexed by
1645	  size-\(d\) subsets \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\). The chart \(U^I\)
1646	  carries the matrix \(X^I\) of size \(d \times r\) whose \(I\)-th \(d \times d\)
1647	  minor is the identity, with the remaining \(d(r-d)\) entries free
1648	  variables (so \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\)). The
1649	  transition maps \(\theta_{I,J} : U^I \cap U^J \to U^J \cap U^I\) defined
1650	  by \(X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) on the locus where \(\det X^I_J\) is
1651	  invertible satisfy the cocycle condition
1652	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\). The glued
1653	  \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec\mathbb{Z}\) is smooth of relative dimension
1654	  \(d(r-d)\). Separatedness is checked from the diagonal
1655	  \(\Delta_{I,J} \subset U^I \times U^J\) cut out by \(X^J_I X^I - X^J = 0\).
1656	  Properness follows from the valuative criterion for DVRs: given
1657	  \(\varphi : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over a DVR \(R\) with fraction
1658	  field \(K\), factoring through some chart \(U^I\) via a ring map
1659	  \(f : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K\), choose \(J\) minimising the valuation
1660	  \(\nu(f(P^I_J))\) of the minor; on the chart \(U^J\) all entries have
1661	  non-negative valuation, so \(f\) extends uniquely to \(\Spec R\).
1662	
1663	  The universal quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\oplus r}
1664	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) is given on \(U^I\) by the matrix \(X^I\)
1665	  and glued by \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I \cap U^J)\). The
1666	  determinant line bundle \(\det\mathcal{U}\) has transition functions
1667	  \(\det g_{I,J} = 1/P^I_J\); the global sections
1668	  \(\sigma_I \in \Gamma(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), \det\mathcal{U})\) defined by
1669	  \(\sigma_I|_{U^J} = P^J_I\) form a base-point-free linear system separating
1670	  points over \(\Spec\mathbb{Z}\), giving an embedding into
1671	  \(\mathbb{P}^{\binom{r}{d}-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}\), which is closed by
1672	  properness. Representability of \(\mathrm{Grass}(r,d)\) is then verified
1673	  on the universal pair \((\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), u)\): a quotient
1674	  \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\oplus r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) of
1675	  rank \(d\) on \(T\) is everywhere locally trivialised; on the locus where
1676	  the columns indexed by \(I\) are a basis the matrix of \(q\) has the form
1677	  \(X^I\), defining the classifying morphism \(T \to U^I \subset
1678	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\). Base change from \(\mathbb{Z}\) to a general noetherian
1679	  base \(S\) with a vector bundle \(V\) is direct: trivialise \(V\) on an open
1680	  cover and glue. For non-trivial \(V\) the same construction produces
1681	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) with Pl\"ucker embedding into
1682	  \(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\).
1683	\end{proof}

1685	\section{Out of scope}
1686	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
1687	
1688	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-foundations layer: the Hilbert
1689	polynomial, the Quot functor, and the Grassmannian scheme with its
1690	representability. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
1691	\begin{itemize}
1692	  \item \textbf{Representability of the Quot scheme} -- the
1693	    Grothendieck--Altman--Kleiman existence theorem and its proof skeleton
1694	    (boundedness, Grassmannian embedding, flattening stratification,
1695	    valuative criterion) are not constructed in this chapter.
1696	  \item \textbf{Flat base change of cohomology} (Stacks 02KH) and its
1697	    associated prerequisites -- not constructed in this chapter.
1698	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
1699	    \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) is the special case
1700	    \(\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\) of the Quot functor and is
1701	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
1702	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
1703	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
1704	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;

1710	    abelian-group-scheme refinement is downstream. Its blueprint home is the
1711	    FGA Picard-representability assembly (the chapter that bundles the relative

1199	[Pullback along an isomorphism is an equivalence of module categories]
1200	  \label{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1201	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1202	  Let \(f : X \to Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes. Then the pullback functor on
1203	  \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is
1204	  one half of an equivalence of categories
1205	  \(Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \simeq X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\), with quasi-inverse
1206	  \((f^{-1})^*\).
1207	\end{lemma}
1208	\begin{proof}
1209	
1210	\leanok
1211	  Assemble the equivalence from the functor \(f^*\) and the candidate inverse
1212	  \((f^{-1})^*\). The unit and counit isomorphisms are built from the pseudofunctor
1213	  coherences of pullback: \((f^{-1})^* \circ f^* \cong (f \circ f^{-1})^* = \mathrm{id}^*
1214	  \cong \mathrm{id}\) and symmetrically, using
1215	  \(f \circ f^{-1} = \mathrm{id}_Y\), \(f^{-1} \circ f = \mathrm{id}_X\)
1216	  (composition-of-pullbacks, congruence under equal maps, and pullback-of-identity).
1217	  The triangle identities are forced by these coherences.
1218	\end{proof}

1262	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}

1264	  \(A\)-module. There is a bundled \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
1265	  \[

1267	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
1268	  \]

1270	  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\); equivalently its inverse sends
1271	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
1272	  hypothesis.

1275	\begin{proof}
1276	  \uses{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv, lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1277	  \leanok
1278	  The underlying \(R'\)-linear equivalence is supplied, already as a bundled
1279	  \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv}\) over \(R'\), by the standalone geometry-free helper
1280	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}, whose construction is now the
1281	  natively \(R'\)-linear pushout-cancellation equivalence of
1282	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}. Crucially, that helper bundles
1283	  \(R'\)-linearity \emph{internally}: there is no hand-written scalar-compatibility
1284	  obligation, no induction over elementary tensors, and in particular \emph{no} residual
1285	  \(r' \cdot 0 = 0\) bookkeeping branch. The only work left to this proof is therefore to
1286	  promote that \(R'\)-linear equivalence to a morphism of \(R'\)-module \emph{objects} in
1287	  the exact extension/restriction-of-scalars packaging that the codomain and domain reads
1288	  produce. We work in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation
1289	  (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to the canonical commutativity isomorphism,
1290	  per the tensor-order convention note).
1291	
1292	  \emph{The lone residual obstruction is the \(A\)-action diamond, resolved at the object
1293	  level.} The pushout-cancellation helper takes its relative tensor product over the
1294	  \emph{canonical} \(A\)-algebra structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\)
1295	  (multiplication in the left factor), whereas the carrier object produced by the
1296	  dictionaries,
1297	  the extension of scalars of \(M\) along the left inclusion \(\iota : A \to A \otimes_R R'\),
1298	  tensors over the \(A\)-module structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\) induced by \(\iota\). These
1299	  two \(A\)-module structures on
1300	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) agree on elements but are distinct as data, so the two relative
1301	  tensor products are distinct types even though their underlying abelian groups and
1302	  their \(R'\)-actions coincide. This diamond is the \emph{only} thing the helper does
1303	  not already discharge, and — because the \(R'\)-linearity is now entirely internal to
1304	  the Mathlib equivalence — it is resolved purely at the carrier level by an
1305	  identity-on-elements \(R'\)-linear bridge
1306	  \[
1307	    eT : (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\operatorname{restr}} M
1308	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1309	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\mathrm{can}} M ,
1310	  \]
1311	  the identity map on generators, which is \(R'\)-linear because both sides carry the
1312	  same \(R'\)-action (restriction of scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\)).
1313	
1314	  Composing the bridge \(eT\) with the natively \(R'\)-linear helper equivalence and
1315	  promoting to an isomorphism of module objects yields the desired bundled
1316	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism of \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\) objects. Being a composite
1317	  of linear equivalences it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of its constituents
1318	  carries a flatness hypothesis.
1319	\end{proof}

1883	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} it suffices to prove that the
1884	  base-change map of Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an

1896	  model, where the base-change map is a morphism \(\alpha\) of quasi-coherent
1897	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R')\)-modules.

1899	  \medskip
1900	  \emph{Passage to global sections.} The functor
1901	  \(\widetilde{(-)} : \operatorname{ModuleCat}(R') \to
1902	  (\operatorname{Spec} R').\mathrm{Modules}\) is fully faithful with essential image

1940	\section{Flat base change for the pushforward}
1941	
1942	The global statement is assembled from the affine lemma and the single homological
1943	input that flatness preserves the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\). That input is
1944	supplied verbatim by Mathlib, recorded here as a dependency anchor.
1945	
1946	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change preserves finite equalizers]
1947	  \label{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
1948	  \lean{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}
1949	  \mathlibok
1950	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1951	  Let \(A \to B\) be a flat ring homomorphism (more generally, let \(B\) be a flat
1952	  \(A\)-module that is also an \(A\)-algebra, with the scalar-tower compatibility),
1953	  and let \(f, g : N \rightrightarrows P\) be a pair of \(A\)-linear maps. Write
1954	  \(\operatorname{eq}(f, g) = \{x \in N : f(x) = g(x)\}\) for their equalizer. Then the
1955	  canonical \(B\)-linear comparison map
1956	  \[
1957	    B \otimes_A \operatorname{eq}(f, g)
1958	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1959	    \operatorname{eq}\bigl(\mathbf{1}_B \otimes f,\ \mathbf{1}_B \otimes g\bigr)
1960	  \]
1961	  is an isomorphism; that is, base change along a flat algebra commutes with the
1962	  formation of the finite equalizer (equivalently, of the kernel: the case
1963	  \(g = 0\) is \texttt{LinearMap.tensorKerEquiv}, underlain by
1964	  \texttt{Module.Flat.ker\_lTensor\_eq} and \texttt{Module.Flat.eqLocus\_lTensor\_eq}).
1965	  Since \(- \otimes_A B\) also preserves finite products, it follows that
1966	  \(- \otimes_A B\) carries the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) equalizer
1967	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows
1968	    \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})\) of a finite cover to the corresponding
1969	  equalizer of the base-changed cover.
1970	\end{lemma}

2129	  \emph{\(H^0\) as a finite equalizer (the sheaf condition).} Choose a finite affine
2130	  open cover \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\) (possible since \(X\) is
2131	  quasi-compact). The sheaf condition for \(\mathcal{F}\) on this cover presents the
2132	  global sections as the equalizer of the two restriction maps
2133	  \[
2134	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})
2135	    \;=\;
2136	    \operatorname{eq}\Bigl(
2137	      \textstyle\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F})
2138	      \;\rightrightarrows\;
2139	      \textstyle\prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})
2140	    \Bigr),
2141	    \qquad U_{ij} = U_i \cap U_j,
2142	  \]
2143	  where the two parallel maps are \(s \mapsto (s_i|_{U_{ij}})\) and
2144	  \(s \mapsto (s_j|_{U_{ij}})\). This is precisely Čech degree \(0\) into degree
2145	  \(1\); it is the sheaf axiom and requires \emph{no} separatedness of \(X\) and no
2146	  higher cohomology.
2147	
2148	  \medskip
2149	  \emph{The separated case.} Suppose first that \(X\) is separated over \(A\). Then
2150	  every intersection \(U_{ij}\) is affine. Base changing the cover yields a finite
2151	  affine cover \(\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \dots \cup (U_t)_B\) of \(X_B\),
2152	  and the same sheaf-condition equalizer presents \(\Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\). The
2153	  affine lemma Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward} (in its
2154	  \(i = 0\)/global-sections form) identifies each base-changed term with the original
2155	  term tensored over \(A\) with \(B\):
2156	  \[
2157	    \Gamma\bigl((U_i)_B, \mathcal{F}_B\bigr) \cong \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B,
2158	    \qquad
2159	    \Gamma\bigl((U_{ij})_B, \mathcal{F}_B\bigr) \cong \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B,
2160	  \]
2161	  compatibly with the two restriction maps. Hence the equalizer diagram for \(X_B\) is
2162	  the diagram for \(X\) with \(- \otimes_A B\) applied (using that \(- \otimes_A B\)
2163	  commutes with the finite products \(\prod_i\), \(\prod_{i,j}\)). Because \(A \to B\)
2164	  is flat, base change commutes with the formation of this finite equalizer
2165	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}):
2166	  \[
2167	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B
2168	    = \operatorname{eq}(\cdots) \otimes_A B
2169	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
2170	    \operatorname{eq}(\cdots \otimes_A B)
2171	    = \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B).
2172	  \]
2173	  This is exactly the comparison map of the reduction step, so it is an isomorphism.
2174	
2175	  \medskip
2176	  \emph{The quasi-separated case.} In general \(X\) need only be quasi-separated. We
2177	  argue by Mayer--Vietoris induction on the number \(t\) of members of a finite affine
2178	  cover \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\), each step reducing to the
2179	  separated/affine case. For \(t = 1\), \(X = U_1\) is affine and the claim is the
2180	  affine lemma. For \(t > 1\), set \(V = U_t\) (affine) and
2181	  \(W = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_{t-1}\) (an open covered by \(t - 1\) affines). Their
2182	  intersection \(W \cap V = \bigcup_{i < t}(U_i \cap U_t)\) is covered by the
2183	  \(t - 1\) opens \(U_i \cap U_t\), each of which is quasi-compact and separated
2184	  (an open of the affine, hence separated, \(U_i\)); thus \(W \cap V\) is separated
2185	  and quasi-compact, and flat base change holds for it by the separated case. The
2186	  two-member cover \(\{W, V\}\) of \(X\) gives the left-exact Mayer--Vietoris
2187	  sequence — itself the sheaf-condition equalizer for that cover —
2188	  \[
2189	    0 \longrightarrow \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})
2190	      \longrightarrow \Gamma(W, \mathcal{F}) \oplus \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})
2191	      \longrightarrow \Gamma(W \cap V, \mathcal{F}),
2192	  \]
2193	  and likewise for \(X_B\) with \(\{W_B, V_B\}\). Flatness of \(A \to B\) makes
2194	  \(- \otimes_A B\) preserve this finite kernel/equalizer
2195	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}); since flat base change holds for
2196	  \(V\) (affine), for \(W\) (inductive hypothesis, \(t - 1\) members), and for
2197	  \(W \cap V\) (separated), it follows for \(X\). In degree \(0\) this gives
2198	  \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \cong \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\), as required.
2199	\end{proof}

[IsScalarTower R S T] [IsScalarTower R R' T] (e : S' ≃ₐ[R'] T)
    (he : e.toRingHom.comp (algebraMap S S') = algebraMap S T) :
    IsPushout R R' S T := by
  rw [isPushout_iff] at h ⊢
  refine IsBaseChange.of_equiv (h.equiv ≪≫ₗ e.toLinearEquiv) fun x ↦ ?_
  simpa [h.equiv_tmul] using DFunLike.congr_fun he x

namespace Algebra

variable (A B : Type*)
  [CommRing A] [CommRing B] [Algebra R A] [Algebra R B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra S B]
  [IsScalarTower R A B] [IsScalarTower R S B] [Algebra.IsPushout R S A B]
variable (M : Type*) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module A M] [IsScalarTower R A M]

/-- (Implementation) If `B = S ⊗[R] A`, this is the canonical `R`-isomorphism:
`B ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[S] S ⊗[R] M`. See `IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` for the `S`-linear version. -/
noncomputable
def IsPushout.cancelBaseChangeAux : B ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[R] S ⊗[R] M :=
  have : IsPushout R A S B := IsPushout.symm inferInstance
  (AlgebraTensorModule.congr ((IsPushout.equiv R A S B).toLinearEquiv).symm
      (LinearEquiv.refl _ _)).restrictScalars R ≪≫ₗ
    (_root_.TensorProduct.comm _ _ _).restrictScalars R ≪≫ₗ
    (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange _ _ A _ _).restrictScalars R ≪≫ₗ
    (_root_.TensorProduct.comm _ _ _).restrictScalars R

@[simp]
lemma IsPushout.cancelBaseChangeAux_symm_tmul (s : S) (m : M) :
    (IsPushout.cancelBaseChangeAux R S A B M).symm (s ⊗ₜ m) = algebraMap S B s ⊗ₜ m := by
  simp [IsPushout.cancelBaseChangeAux, IsPushout.equiv_tmul]

/-- If `B = S ⊗[R] A`, this is the canonical `S`-isomorphism: `B ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[S] S ⊗[R] M`.
This is the cancelling on the left version of
`TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange`. -/
noncomputable
def IsPushout.cancelBaseChange : B ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[S] S ⊗[R] M :=
  LinearEquiv.symm <|
  AddEquiv.toLinearEquiv (IsPushout.cancelBaseChangeAux R S A B M).symm <| by
    intro s x
    induction x with
    | zero => simp
    | add x y hx hy => simp only [smul_add, map_add, hx, hy]
    | tmul s' m => simp [Algebra.smul_def, TensorProduct.smul_tmul']

@[simp]
lemma IsPushout.cancelBaseChange_tmul (m : M) :
    IsPushout.cancelBaseChange R S A B M (1 ⊗ₜ m) = 1 ⊗ₜ m := by
  change ((cancelBaseChangeAux R S A B M).symm).symm (1 ⊗ₜ[A] m) = 1 ⊗ₜ[R] m
  simp [cancelBaseChangeAux, TensorProduct.one_def]

@[simp]
lemma IsPushout.cancelBaseChange_symm_tmul (s : S) (m : M) :
    (IsPushout.cancelBaseChange R S A B M).symm (s ⊗ₜ m) = algebraMap S B s ⊗ₜ m :=
  IsPushout.cancelBaseChangeAux_symm_tmul R S A B M s m

variable (C : Type*) [CommRing C] [Algebra R C] [Algebra A C] [IsScalarTower R A C]

/-- Algebra version of `IsPushout.cancelBaseChange`. -/
noncomputable def IsPushout.cancelBaseChangeAlg : B ⊗[A] C ≃ₐ[S] S ⊗[R] C := by
  refine AlgEquiv.symm
    (AlgEquiv.ofLinearEquiv (IsPushout.cancelBaseChange R S A B C).symm ?_ ?_)
  · simp [TensorProduct.one_def]

content:
/-- **Seam 2: the pushforward pseudofunctor reindex of the inner comparison.** In the generic
pullback square with legs `g' = pullback.fst`, `f' = pullback.snd`, write `θ_in` for the inner
composite built from the `(g')`-unit and the three pushforward pseudofunctor coherences
(`pushforwardComp` twice, `pushforwardCongr` once) that appear inside `pushforwardBaseChangeMap`
before the `(g^* ⊣ g_*)`-transpose. Read on global sections over `Spec R` through the
`Γ`-pushforward dictionaries (`gammaPushforwardTildeIso` / `gammaPushforwardIso`), with the
codomain pinned by `base_change_mate_codomain_read`, the map `θ_in` is the canonical `R`-linear map
`ρ : m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m` (`base_change_mate_inner_value`), i.e. `restrictScalars ψ` of the
codomain-read transport of Seam 1's unit value. See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex`. -/
theorem base_change_mate_fstar_reindex {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
    (gammaPushforwardTildeIso φ M).inv ≫
      (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
                (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).unit.app (tilde M)) ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp
              (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)) (Spec.map φ)).hom.app _ ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
              (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w).hom.app _ ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp
              (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)) (Spec.map ψ)).inv.app _) ≫
      (gammaPushforwardIso ψ
            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
                (tilde M))) ≪≫
          (ModuleCat.restrictScalars ψ.hom).mapIso (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M)).hom
      = base_change_mate_inner_value ψ φ M := by
  -- REMAINING (the pseudofunctor reindex): identify the generic-square legs with the `Spec`-maps of
  -- the tensor inclusions `ι_A : A → A ⊗_R R'`, `ι_{R'} : R' → A ⊗_R R'` via
  -- `pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor` (through `pullbackSpecIso`). Under this identification the
  -- `(g')`-unit factor of `inner` becomes the `(Spec ι_A)`-unit, whose `Γ`-section value is the
  -- algebraic unit `η_M : m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m` by Seam 1 (`base_change_mate_unit_value`). The three
  -- pushforward coherences (`pushforwardComp` ×2, `pushforwardCongr`) merely rebracket the iterated
  -- `f_*` along the two factorizations `(g' ≫ f)_* = (f' ≫ g)_*` of the square's composite, which on
  -- global sections is functoriality of `restrictScalars` and introduces no new content. Composing,
  -- the `Spec R`-section reading of `inner` is `restrictScalars ψ` of the codomain-read transport of
  -- the unit value, i.e. `base_change_mate_inner_value`. Uses: `base_change_mate_unit_value` (Seam 1),
  -- `base_change_mate_codomain_read`, `pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor`, `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`.
  sorry

/-- **Seam 3: the `(g^* ⊣ g_*)` transpose of the comparison on sections.** By the counit formula
for the `(g^* ⊣ g_*)`-adjunction (`g = Spec ψ`; Mathlib's `Adjunction.homEquiv_counit`),
`pushforwardBaseChangeMap` factors as `g^*(inner)` followed by the counit, with no opaque
adjunction transpose remaining. On the global sections over `Spec R'`, conjugated by the domain
read `Θ_src` and codomain read `Θ_tgt`, this transpose is the extension of scalars along
`ψ : R → R'` of the `Spec R`-section reading `ρ` of `inner` (Seam 2); on the generator `r' ⊗ m`
it returns `(1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`, exactly the inverse of the regrouping isomorphism
`base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`. See blueprint `lem:base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`. -/
theorem base_change_mate_gstar_transpose {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
    (base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M).inv ≫
      (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).map
            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).map
                ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
                    (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).unit.app (tilde M)) ≫
              (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp
                  (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)) (Spec.map φ)).hom.app _ ≫
              (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
                  (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w).hom.app _ ≫
              (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp
                  (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)) (Spec.map ψ)).inv.app _) ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map ψ)).counit.app
            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))).obj
                (tilde M)))) ≫
      (base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M).hom
      = (base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv := by
  -- PARTIAL: split `Γ(g^*(inner) ≫ ε_g) = Γ(g^*(inner)) ≫ Γ(ε_g)` by functoriality of
  -- `moduleSpecΓFunctor`. After this the goal is
  --   `Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ (Γ(g^*(inner)) ≫ Γ(ε_g)) ≫ Θ_tgt = regroupEquiv.inv`.
  rw [Functor.map_comp]
  -- REMAINING (the genuine crux): the pullback-dictionary coherence. Conjugating
  -- `Γ(g^*(inner)) ≫ Γ(ε_g)` by `Θ_src = base_change_mate_domain_read` and
  -- `Θ_tgt = base_change_mate_codomain_read` replaces `g^* = (Spec ψ)^*` of a tilde by extension
  -- of scalars along `ψ` (Lemma `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`), turning it into
  -- `extendScalars ψ` (≡ `LinearMap.lTensor R'`) applied to the `Spec R`-section reading `ρ` of
  -- `inner` supplied by Seam 2 (`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex`, RHS = `base_change_mate_inner_value`).
  -- Since `ρ : m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`, its `R'`-base change sends `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`, which is
  -- `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv.inv` on generators (both `R'`-linear, `ext` on `1 ⊗ m`). The
  -- coherence is the `pullback_spec_tilde_iso ψ` / counit naturality identification of the conjugated
  -- `g^*`-leg with `extendScalars ψ ∘ ρ`; it is the Mathlib-absent mate-unwinding over the generic
  -- pullback square. Uses: `base_change_mate_fstar_reindex` (Seam 2), `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`.
  sorry

/-- **The section-level base-change map is the base change of the unit.** With the domain and
codomain pinned by `base_change_mate_domain_read` (`Θ_src`) and `base_change_mate_codomain_read`
(`Θ_tgt`), the conjugated section-level base-change map
`Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(θ) ≫ Θ_tgt : R' ⊗_R M ⟶ (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` is the `R'`-base change of the algebraic
unit `η_M : M → (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M`, `m ↦ (1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`: on the generator `r' ⊗ m` it returns
`r' • ((1 ⊗ 1) ⊗ m) = (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`. Equivalently it equals the *inverse* of the regrouping
isomorphism `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`, i.e.
`Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(θ) ≫ Θ_tgt = (base_change_mate_regroupEquiv ψ φ M).inv`. See blueprint
`lem:base_change_mate_section_identity`.

The argument is carried out directly on the module of global sections; no abstract adjoint-mate
identification at the level of sheaves intervenes. By `ModuleCat` hom-extensionality it suffices to
check on the `R'`-module generators `1 ⊗ₜ x` (`ext x`), leaving the concrete per-generator identity
`(Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(θ) ≫ Θ_tgt) (1 ⊗ x) = regroupEquiv.inv (1 ⊗ x)`. By construction `Γ(θ)` is, read on

Now I have definitive proof: Seam 2 (`base_change_mate_fstar_reindex`) and Seam 3 (`base_change_mate_gstar_transpose`) both end with `sorry` in the Lean file. The blueprint has `\leanok` on the statement (declaration exists in Lean) but NOT on the proof block (proof not closed).

Also important: Seam 3 has `rw [Functor.map_comp]` applied before the `sorry` — so there's a partial tactic start but the proof isn't complete.

input.description: Read the Module.Finite hypothesis NOTE in QuotScheme.tex

content:
\emph{Reduction to generation in degree \(1\).} Since \(R\) is Noetherian and
  finitely generated as a \(\kappa\)-algebra, it is generated by finitely many
  homogeneous elements of positive degree. Replacing \(R\) by a Veronese
  subalgebra \(R^{(e)} = \bigoplus_n R_{en}\) for a suitable \(e\) and re-grading
  (and \(M\) by the corresponding Veronese module), we may assume \(R\) is
  generated by its degree-one part, \(\kappa[R_1] = R\). Then \(R\) is a graded
  quotient of a polynomial ring \(\kappa[x_1, \dots, x_r]\) with the \(x_i\) of
  degree \(1\); we induct on the minimal number \(r\) of degree-one generators.
  % NOTE: The existence of finitely many degree-one generators (the number r the
  % induction is on) is expected to follow from the standing hypotheses --
  % "finitely generated κ-algebra" together with "generated in degree 1" -- so no
  % new hypothesis on gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational is anticipated. To be
  % confirmed when the prover formalizes the G-assembly: degree-1 generation gives
  % R₁ as a finite-dimensional κ-vector space spanning R as a κ-algebra, and a
  % κ-basis of R₁ is a finite degree-one generating set.

  \emph{Base case \(r = 0\).} Here \(R = \kappa\) and \(M\) is a finitely
  generated \(\kappa\)-module, hence finite-dimensional, so \(M_n = 0\) and
  \(\dim_\kappa M_n = 0\) for all \(n \gg 0\). The Hilbert series
  \(\sum_n (\dim_\kappa M_n) X^n\) is therefore a polynomial; taking \(d = 0\)
  and \(p \in \mathbb{Q}[X]\) the polynomial with these coefficients gives
  \(\dim_\kappa M_n = [X^n]\bigl(p \cdot (1 - X)^{0}\bigr)\) for all \(n\).

  \emph{Inductive step.} Let \(x = x_r \in R_1\) be one of a minimal set of
  degree-one generators. The quotient ring \(R/(x)\) is itself a graded quotient
  of \(\kappa[x_1, \dots, x_{r-1}]\), hence an \((r-1)\)-generated graded
  \(\kappa\)-algebra to which the induction hypothesis applies; that \(R/(x)\) is a
  graded ring with degree-zero part \(\kappa\) is the content of \(G_3\)
  (\cref{lem:graded_quotientRing_gradedRing}), using that \((x)\) is homogeneous
  (\cref{lem:ideal_homogeneous_span_mathlib}).

input.description: Read gf_torsion_reindex proof in Lean

content:
/-- **L5b — torsion reindex onto fewer variables** (`lem:gf_torsion_reindex`,
Nitsure §4 support-dimension drop). For `A` a noetherian domain, `d ≥ 1`, and `T`
a finite torsion module over `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exist `g ≠ 0` in `A` and
`m' < d` such that, after inverting `g`, the localisation `T_g` is module-finite
over `A_g[X_1,…,X_{m'}]` (one may take `m' = d - 1`). -/
theorem gf_torsion_reindex
    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
    (d : ℕ) (hd : 0 < d) (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T]
    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
    [Module A T] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
    ∃ (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0) (m' : ℕ) (_ : m' < d)
      (_ : Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
      (_ : Module (Localization.Away g) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T))
      (_ : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
              (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T)),
      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m') (Localization.Away g))
        (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) T) := by
  -- Reshape `d = m + 1` (uses `hd : 0 < d`); the distinguished eliminated variable is `X₀`.
  obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero hd.ne'
  -- L5b.1 (`gf_torsion_annihilator`): a non-zero annihilator `F` of the torsion module `T`.
  obtain ⟨F, hF0, hFann⟩ := gf_torsion_annihilator A (m + 1) T htors
  -- L5b.2 (`gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`): a triangular `A`-algebra automorphism `e` and a
  -- non-zero `g ∈ A` making `e F` monic in `X₀` up to a unit after inverting `g`.
  obtain ⟨g, hg0, e, hunit⟩ := gf_nagata_monic_lastVar A m F hF0
  -- L5b.3 (`mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar`): the coefficient subring
  -- `A_g[X₁,…,X_m] = MvPolynomial (Fin m) A_g` has `A_g[X₀,…,X_m]/(map (e F))` module-finite
  -- over it; this is the source of the dimension drop `m' = m < m + 1`.
  have hfin := mvPolynomial_quotient_finite_of_monic_lastVar (Localization.Away g) m
    (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away g)) (e F)) hunit
  classical
  -- ASSEMBLY. Strategy: work with the *`P`-localisation* `Tg' := LocalizedModule (C(powers g)) T`
  -- of `T` (it inherits the full `P_g`-module API, finiteness, and quotient structure for free),
  -- prove `Tg'` module-finite over `R := MvPolynomial (Fin m) A_g`, and finally transport the
  -- module structures + finiteness to the goal's `T_g := LocalizedModule (powers g) T` (the
  -- `A`-localisation) along the canonical `A_g`-linear equivalence `T_g ≃ₗ[A_g] Tg'`.
  -- Notation: `P := MvPolynomial (Fin (m+1)) A`, `P_g := MvPolynomial (Fin (m+1)) A_g`,
  --           `A_g := Localization.Away g`, `MC := Submonoid.map C (powers g) ⊆ P`.
  letI algPPg : Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A)
      (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g)) :=
    (MvPolynomial.algebraMvPolynomial :
      Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g)))
  set MC : Submonoid (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) :=
    Submonoid.map (MvPolynomial.C) (Submonoid.powers g) with hMC_def
  haveI hPgloc : IsLocalization MC (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g)) :=
    MvPolynomial.isLocalization (Submonoid.powers g) (Localization.Away g)
  -- The `P`-localised torsion module and its canonical `P_g`-module structure.
  letI hmodPg : Module (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g))

letI hmodPg : Module (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g))
      (LocalizedModule MC T) :=
    LocalizedModule.moduleOfIsLocalization
  haveI htowerPg : IsScalarTower (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A)
      (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g)) (LocalizedModule MC T) :=
    inferInstance
  -- `Tg'` is module-finite over `P_g` (localisation of the `P`-finite module `T`).
  haveI hfinPg : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g))
      (LocalizedModule MC T) :=
    Module.Finite.of_isLocalizedModule MC (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap MC T)
  -- `Fg := algebraMap P Pg F` annihilates `Tg'` (localising `F • T = 0`).
  set Fg : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g) :=
    algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) (MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) (Localization.Away g)) F
    with hFg_def
  have hFgann : ∀ y : LocalizedModule MC T, Fg • y = 0 := by
    intro y
    induction y using LocalizedModule.induction_on with
    | _ t s =>
      have hFt : F • t = 0 := Module.mem_annihilator.mp hFann t
      rw [hFg_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul, LocalizedModule.smul'_mk, hFt,
        LocalizedModule.zero_mk]

-- instances, so the IH has the shape
  --   IH : ∀ m < d, ∀ (N : Type _) [AddCommGroup N] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N]
  --          [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N] [Module A N]
  --          [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N],
  --          ∃ f ≠ 0, Module.Free (A_f) (N_f).
  induction d using Nat.strong_induction_on generalizing A N with
  | _ d IH =>
    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos d with hd | hd
    · -- Base case `d = 0`: `MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`, so `N` is module-finite
      -- over `A` itself (via `Module.Finite.trans` through the iso), and the claim
      -- is the finite-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`.
      subst hd
      haveI : Module.Finite A (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) :=
        Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv A (Fin 0)).symm.toLinearEquiv
      haveI : Module.Finite A N := Module.Finite.trans (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) N
      exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A N
    · -- Inductive step. Pass to `K = Frac A` and split on whether `N_K = 0`.
      by_cases htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) N)
      · -- Torsion sub-case `N_K = 0`: closed by the L1 torsion base case applied
        -- with the finite-type polynomial ring `B := A[X_1,…,X_d]` as the module's
        -- ring of definition.
        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N htors
      · -- Generic-rank dévissage (the genuine generic-rank residue): with `N_K ≠ 0`,
        -- let `m` be the generic rank of `N` over the domain `A[X_1,…,X_d]`; choosing
        -- `m` elements whose images form a `K(X)`-basis and clearing denominators
        -- gives, after inverting some `g ≠ 0`, a short exact sequence
        --   `0 → A_g[X]^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0`
        -- with `T` torsion of support dimension `< d`. After a Noether-normalisation
        -- reindex `T` is finite over a polynomial ring in `m' < d` variables, so the
        -- IH applies:  `IH m' (by …) T` yields `h ≠ 0` with `T_h` free over `A_h`.
        -- The free middle term `A_g[X]^{⊕m}` localises to a free `A_{gh}`-module by
        -- the `d = 0` leaf (`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`) applied
        -- coordinatewise, and `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3)
        -- splices the localised SES to give `N_f` free over `A_f` with `f := g·h`.
        --
        -- The IH is now genuinely in scope and quantifies over the base domain `A`
        -- (the structural fix of this iter): `IH m' (hm' : m' < d) A_g T_g` typechecks
        -- at the reindexed base `A_g = Localization.Away g`.
        --
        -- The generic-rank SES is now concretely available from `gf_generic_rank_ses`:
        obtain ⟨_mrank, _φ, _hφinj, _hTtors⟩ := gf_generic_rank_ses A d N
        -- `φ : (Fin mrank → P_d) →ₗ[P_d] N` is injective with torsion cokernel
        -- `T := N ⧸ range φ` (`hTtors`). The remaining residue, to be assembled once
        -- `gf_torsion_reindex` (L5b, still `sorry`) lands, is:
        --   1. equip `T` with its restricted `A`-module structure + scalar tower
        --      (the restriction-of-scalars motive plumbing) and `Module.Finite P_d T`;
        --   2. `gf_torsion_reindex A d hd T hTtors` ⟹ `g ≠ 0`, `m' < d`, and `T_g`
        --      module-finite over `A_g[X_1,…,X_{m'}]`;
        --   3. `IH m' hm' (Localization.Away g) T_g` ⟹ `h ≠ 0` in `A_g` with `(T_g)_h`
        --      free over `(A_g)_h` (the IH is now available AT BASE `A_g`);
        --   4. descend the witness `h = a/g^k` to `f := g·a ∈ A` via
        --      `free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul` (L3b);
        --   5. the free left end `P_d^{⊕mrank}` localises to a free `A_f`-module, and
        --      `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3) splices the localised
        --      SES `0 → (P_d^{⊕mrank})_f → N_f → T_f → 0` to give `N_f` free over `A_f`.
        -- Steps 1–5 are blocked only on `gf_torsion_reindex`; the SES (step 0) and the
        -- base-domain-generalised IH are both in hand.
        sorry

theorem exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial

    (d : ℕ) (N : Type u) [AddCommGroup N]

    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧

  -- apply to the lower-support-dimension torsion quotient `T` (a module over a
  -- polynomial ring in fewer variables `m < d`), and the module instances depend
  -- on `d`, so a faithful induction must re-quantify `N` over each `d`. The

(htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
    ∃ F : MvPolynomial (Fin d) A, F ≠ 0 ∧
      F ∈ Module.annihilator (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T := by

/-! ### L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables (`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`)

The single-variable elimination of the support-dimension drop needs a triangular
change of variables `e` making a non-zero `F ∈ A[X_0,…,X_n]` monic in `X_0` up to a
unit after inverting one `g ∈ A`. Mathlib's Noether-normalisation development
(`Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization`) constructs exactly this transformation
`T` and proves the leading coefficient is a unit — but only over a *field*, and as
`private` lemmas. The block below replays that construction over a noetherian *domain*
(the field is used in Mathlib only to conclude the top coefficient is a unit; over a
domain it is merely non-zero, and becomes a unit after inverting it). The degree
bookkeeping (`degreeOf_zero_t`, `leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t`, `T_leadingcoeff_eq`) is
a domain-adapted transcription of the Mathlib originals. -/

section NagataNormalization

-- `AlgebraicGeometry.Polynomial` exists and would shadow the root namespace, so the
-- `Polynomial`/`MvPolynomial` lemma APIs are opened with explicit `_root_.` prefixes.
open _root_.Polynomial _root_.MvPolynomial _root_.Ideal Nat _root_.RingHom List

/-- `finSuccEquiv` commutes with coefficient base change `MvPolynomial.map phi`. -/
private theorem finSuccEquiv_map_comm {A B : Type*} [CommRing A] [CommRing B]
    (phi : A →+* B) (m : ℕ) (q : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) :
    finSuccEquiv B m (MvPolynomial.map phi q)
      = Polynomial.map (MvPolynomial.map phi) (finSuccEquiv A m q) := by
  induction q using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
  | C a => simp [finSuccEquiv_apply]
  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
  | mul_X p i hp =>
    simp only [map_mul, Polynomial.map_mul, hp]
    congr 1
    cases i using Fin.cases with
    | zero => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_zero]
    | succ j => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ]

variable {k : Type*} [CommRing k] [IsDomain k] {n : ℕ} (f : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k)
variable (v w : Fin (n + 1) →₀ ℕ)

local notation3 "up" => 2 + f.totalDegree
local notation3 "r" => fun (i : Fin (n + 1)) ↦ up ^ i.1

omit [IsDomain k] in
variable {f v} in
private lemma lt_up (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) : ∀ l ∈ ofFn v, l < up := by grind

/-- The triangular Nagata substitution `X_i ↦ X_i + c • X_0 ^ (up ^ i)` (`i ≠ 0`),
`X_0 ↦ X_0`. Transcribed from `Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization` to a domain. -/
private noncomputable abbrev T1 (c : k) :
    MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k →ₐ[k] MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k :=
  aeval fun i ↦ if i = 0 then X 0 else X i + c • X 0 ^ r i

omit [IsDomain k] in
private lemma t1_comp_t1_neg (c : k) : (T1 f c).comp (T1 f (-c)) = AlgHom.id _ _ := by
  rw [comp_aeval, ← MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]; ext i v; cases i using Fin.cases <;> simp

/-- The Nagata transformation as an `A`-algebra automorphism. -/
private noncomputable abbrev T := AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom (T1 f 1) (T1 f (-1))
  (t1_comp_t1_neg f 1) (by simpa using t1_comp_t1_neg f (-1))

omit [IsDomain k] in
private lemma sum_r_mul_ne (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) (wlt : ∀ i, w i < up) (ne : v ≠ w) :
    ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * v x ≠ ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * w x := by
  intro h
  refine ne <| Finsupp.ext <| congrFun <| ofFn_inj.mp ?_
  apply ofDigits_inj_of_len_eq (Nat.lt_add_right f.totalDegree one_lt_two)
    (by simp) (lt_up vlt) (lt_up wlt)
  simpa only [ofDigits_eq_sum_mapIdx, mapIdx_eq_ofFn, get_ofFn, length_ofFn,
    Fin.val_cast, mul_comm, sum_ofFn] using h

private lemma degreeOf_zero_t {a : k} (ha : a ≠ 0) : ((T f) (monomial v a)).degreeOf 0 =
    ∑ i : Fin (n + 1), (r i) * v i := by
  rw [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv, monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_pow v fun a ↦ X a]
  simp only [Fin.prod_univ_succ, Fin.sum_univ_succ, map_mul, map_prod, map_pow,
    AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, MvPolynomial.aeval_C, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, if_pos, Fin.succ_ne_zero,

Now I can see that `T1` and `T` are defined in the FlatteningStratification.lean file itself (as `private noncomputable abbrev`s). They appear at lines ~695 and ~702. These are private and don't map to blueprint decls directly — they're implementation details for `gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`.

Looking at the blueprint, `def:gf_nagata_T1` and `def:gf_nagata_T` are listed separately in the blueprint as blueprint nodes, but they correspond to these private Lean definitions. Since they're private, leandag can't match them.
The Nagata machinery itself is fully formalized with no sorries across the helper functions. The real gaps for GF are `gf_noether_clear_denominators` which lacks Mathlib support, and `gf_polynomial_core` where the inductive step has a sorry that depends on other missing pieces.

However, the blueprint is missing the `\leanok` marker for it—this looks like a sync gap where the declaration got proved but the marker wasn't added, which I should flag as something to fix. I can see that `lem:base_change_mate_unit_value` has `\leanok` on both the statement and proof blocks in the blueprint, so that one is properly marked. But `lem:gf_torsion_reindex` is missing the marker entirely despite being proof-complete—this is the discrepancy that `sync_leanok` should have caught and fixed.   \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (base case).}
  Since \(K \otimes_A M = S^{-1}M\) with \(S = A \setminus \{0\}\), the hypothesis
  \(M_K = 0\) means every element of \(M\) is killed by some non-zero element of
  \(A\). Choose a finite \(B\)-generating set \(v_1, \dots, v_k\) of \(M\) (which
  exists because \(M\) is a finite \(B\)-module), and for each \(i\) an

  \(f := a_1 \cdots a_k\) is non-zero (\(A\) is a domain) and annihilates every
  generator, hence \(f M = 0\). Therefore \(M_f = 0\), which is free over \(A_f\).

    (A B M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]

    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧

    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul B a (b • x), ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul B a x,
